title: Creamy Walnut Stuffed Vegetables
description: A nutty and creamy Greek-inspired meal-prep dish featuring cooking wine, dumplings, pescatarian-friendly options.

ingredients.name: Potatoes
ingredients.notes: Waxy potatoes work best for this recipe

ingredients.name: Carrots
ingredients.notes: Orange or white varieties are preferred

ingredients.name: Dumplings
ingredients.notes: Any type of dumplings can be used, such as wontons or ravioli

ingredients.name: Butter
ingredients.notes: For a creamy base in the sauce

ingredients.name: Garlic Powder
ingredients.notes: To enhance flavor without using fresh garlic

ingredients.name: White Wine or Cooking Wine
ingredients.notes: To reduce and create a sauce

instructions: Slice potatoes and carrots into rounds about 1/4-inch thick.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add onions and sauté until translucent, about 2 minutes.
Add garlic powder and cook for another minute.
Place potato and carrot slices in an even layer on a baking sheet. Distribute dumplings evenly among the vegetables.
Pour white wine or cooking wine into the skillet with the remaining ingredients. Reduce to thicken and create sauce.
Combine reduced sauce with heavy cream, butter, and chopped pistachios (if using). Mix well and pour over the vegetable-filled dumplings.
Bake for about 25-30 minutes until vegetables are tender and filling is bubbly and golden brown.
Let sit for a few minutes before serving to allow flavors to meld together.
